• NASA will roll the largest section of the agency’s SLS (Space Launch System) rocket, which will launch the second crewed Artemis mission, out of the agency’s Michoud Assembly Facility in New Orleans on Monday,….

articles.title: Karnataka’s financial health is stronger than that of Central Government: CM Siddaramaiah
articles.summary:
• The Chief Minister said that when Narendra Modi assumed office as Prime Minister in 2014, the country’s total outstanding loans stood at ₹53.11 lakh crore.

articles.title: ‘Needs to be examined’: Supreme Court seeks Centre, ECI response on biometric voting plea
articles.summary:
• The Supreme Court is reviewing a proposal to implement fingerprint and iris scans at polling stations to combat voter fraud and impersonation.

articles.title: ‘Deeply flawed’: Sonia Gandhi slams women’s quota bill, calls it ‘assault on Constitution’
articles.summary:
• Sonia Gandhi asserts the Centre's focus on delimitation, not women's reservation, is the "real issue" behind the special Parliament session, deeming it "extremely dangerous" and an "assault" on the Constitution.
• She argues population control performers shouldn't be disadvantaged and criticizes the government's rushed approach for political gain, calling it "hollow" and "anti-democratic.".

articles.title: What if Islamabad fires? IAF plans radar that can track drones, rockets from 450 km away – Know features
articles.summary:
• The Indian Air Force (IAF) is preparing to strengthen its air defence network with a new long-range radar system, which is capable of tracking incoming missiles, drones and aircraft from distances of over 450….
• The system is expected to improve early warning capability and help detect aerial threats even before they approach Indian airspace.

articles.title: NASA Awards Data Engineering, Informatics Support Contract
articles.summary:
• NASA has selected Development Seed of Washington to provide research and development services to the Office of Data Science and Informatics (ODSI) at the agency’s Marshall Space Flight Center in Huntsville,….
• The award is a performance-based, indefinite-delivery/indefinite-quantity contract with a maximum potential value of $76 million.
• A phase-in period begins on May 15, 2026, […].

articles.title: Oil shortage, price spike & more: How Hormuz blockade is hitting India and global markets
articles.summary:
• Global markets are in turmoil following President Trump's announcement of a naval blockade in the Strait of Hormuz.
• This move has sent crude oil prices soaring and threatens to isolate Iran, potentially triggering further escalation.
• India faces significant economic challenges, including a ballooning energy import bill and disruptions to its exports and workforce remittances.

articles.title: Make draft bills public, say civil society members
articles.summary:
• Social activists, academics, and former civil servants have urged the government to publicly share draft bills on women's reservation and delimitation for wider consultation.
• While wholeheartedly supporting women's reservation, the group expressed concern over the "opaque and non-consultative manner" of their introduction in the upcoming special parliamentary session.

articles.title: Kuwait Citizenship Law Amendments 2026: Decree expands revocation powers; naturalisation rules tighten
articles.summary:
• Kuwait has enacted significant changes to its citizenship law, empowering the government to grant and revoke nationality with greater authority.
• The new decree tightens eligibility criteria, particularly for naturalisation and marriage-based applications, while reinforcing strict rules against dual nationality.
• These reforms aim to safeguard national identity and address past irregularities, ensuring citizenship is granted strictly by law.

articles.title: Global banks play hedge card after RBI blow on rupee bets
articles.summary:
• Foreign banks are reportedly reclassifying arbitrage deals, impacted by RBI's rupee speculation clampdown, as hedges for capital from overseas parents.
• This move aims to circumvent the $100 million net open position limit imposed by the regulator.
• The RBI may scrutinize these reclassifications based on timelines and documentation.

articles.title: Who to be next Bihar CM? BJP likely to declare face for top post on Tuesday
articles.summary:
• The leadership change will mark a milestone for the BJP, which is set to get its first chief minister in Bihar, the only Hindi heartland state where the top post has so far eluded the party.
• Even so, party leaders have remained cautious in their public remarks.
• Dilip Jaiswal, a minister and former Bihar BJP president, said, "It is an emotional moment for us.
• We hope that Nitish Kumar will continue to offer his guidance to the new government.".

articles.title: Stock markets plunge as failure of U.S.–Iran negotiations fuels concerns of prolonged conflict
articles.summary:
• The 30-share BSE Sensex tumbled 1,613.09 points to 75,937.16 in early trade.

articles.title: Petrol, diesel rates on 13 April — Check fuel prices today in Delhi, Mumbai, Chennai, Kolkata, Bengaluru & other cities
articles.summary:

articles.title: 'Mood favouring the BJP': PM Modi after interacting with party workers in West Bengal, takes jab at TMC

articles.title: Assam government moves Supreme Court against transit bail granted to Pawan Khera
articles.summary:

articles.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 13 April: US-Iran talks falter; Strait of Hormuz uncertainty deepens
articles.summary:
• Weekend developments have raised fresh concerns worldwide after talks between the United States and Iran on Saturday ended without agreement, fuelling uncertainty and raising tensions once again.
• The planned US blockade targeting Iranian ports going into effect has added to fears of further escalation and global trade disruption.
• Meanwhile, the humanitarian toll mounts, with Lebanese authorities reporting that deaths from Israeli airstrikes has now surpassed 2,000.
• Stay with us for live updates on humanitarian impacts and diplomatic efforts.
• App users can follow coverage here.

articles.title: T.N. Assembly election: Jayalalithaa would have opposed delimitation if she were alive, says Stalin

articles.title: 'Trust deficit in Ladakh': Sonam Wangchuk says no date for next round of talks with government announced

articles.title: 'Crucial to ensure transparency': Kerala assembly opposition leader Satheesan urges EC to release poll data

articles.title: Before women's quota bill session, PM Modi steps up direct outreach

• This direct outreach seeks to build public backing amidst opposition concerns regarding the delimitation process.

articles.title: BJP issues whip, orders full attendance of MPs for April 16–18 special Parliament session
articles.summary:

• The special session is expected to focus on the Women's Reservation Amendment Bill, with the government proposing to use 2011 census data for implementation timelines.

articles.title: With 10.9% net voter deletions, Bengal 5th among 12 states, UTs

• While additions were lowest among major states, West Bengal led in deletions through adjudication.

articles.title: Elections 2026 live updates: 'TVK is BJP’s B-team,’ says DMK candidate ahead of Tamil Nadu polls

articles.title: 'Baseless narratives cannot alter reality': India rejects China's renaming of  places in Arunachal Pradesh

articles.title: Tamil Nadu assembly polls: Udhayanidhi Stalin launches campaign in Mannargudi

articles.title: Tamil Nadu Assembly elections: How actively does Chennai vote? A look at the trends

• In the elections that followed, the turnout dropped, and has since remained largely unchanged, staying in the range of 61%-62%.

articles.title: Pak military contingent lands in Saudi Arabia

• This deployment, part of a strategic defence pact, aims to bolster joint military coordination and operational readiness.

articles.title: Bengal assembly elections: Over 3.6 crore voters eligible for first phase polls on April 23, says EC

articles.title: $1bn, a bride or a breakup: Uganda general’s bizarre ultimatum to Turkey

articles.title: FPI profile: Foreign investors continue offloading in April, pull out Rs 48,213 crore so far

articles.title: ‘Mischievous attempts’: India slams China over assigning ‘fictitious’ names to places in Arunachal Pradesh

articles.title: Justice Varma saves himself from ignoble first

articles.title: Artemis II Splashes Down

• The Artemis II crew accomplished many milestones on their nearly 10-day mission, surpassing the Apollo 13 record for farthest crewed spaceflight and capturing views of the far side of the Moon.

articles.title: Apple at 50: A loyalist on the brand’s evolution in India

articles.title: Kamal Kharazi, former Iranian foreign minister, dies week after he was injured in US-Israeli strikes

• He was 81 and head of the Strategic Council for International Relations.

articles.title: EC rejects TMC pick's nomination for subsisting contract with govt

articles.title: Falling emissions intensity masks India’s rising absolute output
articles.summary:
• While the country has successfully reduced emissions intensity, and increased non-fossil power capacity, challenges remain in translating these achievements into absolute emissions reductions.
• The next few years will be critical in addressing these gaps and ensuring a sustainable future.

articles.title: Lok Sabha clears tweak to bill on trans persons amid opposition protests
articles.summary:
• Lok Sabha approved the Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Amendment Bill 2026 via voice vote, despite opposition calls for wider consultations.
• The government stated the changes aim to ensure benefits reach genuine transgender individuals, moving away from self-perceived gender identity and introducing a medical board for certification.

articles.title: 3 Ways Students Can Get Involved With Artemis
articles.summary:

articles.title: Combine view for all mandates in one place: RBI to payment industry
articles.summary:

articles.title: NASA PC-12 Aircraft Makes Move to Support Flight Research Across Agency
articles.summary:
• The PC-12 was acquired in 2022 by NASA’s Glenn Research Center in Cleveland for use in advanced technology development.

articles.title: Government calls all-party meet today to address concerns over West Asia crisis
articles.summary:
• Following Prime Minister Modi's statements on the West Asia conflict, the government has convened an all-party meeting to foster a united parliamentary voice.
• The meeting aims to address opposition concerns and present a unified stance on the escalating crisis, which impacts India's energy supply and its large diaspora in the Gulf.

articles.title: NASA Research Proposes Technology to Seek Earth-Like Exoplanets
articles.summary:
• As NASA seeks to understand the mysteries of the universe, the agency is advancing technologies to locate and explore Earth-like planets far beyond our solar system.
• A key element of this research involves observing reflected light from exoplanets, which can reveal indicators of Earth-like features such as water and oxygen.

articles.title: Government plans to merge Ircon, RVNL to help them scale up
articles.summary:
• Two major railway PSUs, Rail Vikas Nigam (RVNL) and Ircon International, are set to merge to create a larger entity capable of undertaking significant domestic and international projects.
• This strategic move aims to eliminate duplication, pool resources, and enhance the combined company's ability to bid for mega infrastructure projects, with a combined order book exceeding Rs 1.5 lakh crore.

articles.title: Lok Sabha passes Bill redefining 'transgender'; excludes sexual orientations, self-perceived identities
articles.summary:
• The bill aims to protect a specific social and cultural group facing extreme discrimination, with new provisions for authority designation and graded punishments for offenses.
• Opposition members criticized the bill for limiting self-determination.

articles.title: Budget Session: Narendra Modi govt plans bills to roll out women’s quota before delimitation:  What will change?
articles.summary:
• The Modi government plans to introduce two crucial bills in Parliament to ensure the women's reservation law is implemented before the delimitation exercise, aiming for increased female representation in the….
• Could this milestone change the political landscape by 2029?.

articles.title: Israel to occupy southern Lebanon, create 'security zone'
articles.summary:
• Lebanon responded by expelling Iran's ambassador, citing diplomatic violations.
• This move escalates regional tensions, with France urging Israel to avoid a wider ground operation while supporting Lebanon's diplomatic action.

articles.title: Lok Sabha passes Digital Personal Data Protection Amendment Bill 2026
articles.summary:
• Bill amends DPDP Act 2023 and establishes Data Protection Board of India.
• Citizens get right to correction, erasure and grievance redressal of personal data.
• Significant processing entities must appoint Data Protection Officer.
• Children under 18 need parental consent for data processing.
• Non-compliance penalty up to Rs 250 crore.
• The Bill was passed with 312 votes in favour and 78 against.

articles.title: DRDO successfully tests Agni-VI ICBM with range over 10,000 km
articles.summary:
• MIRV technology enabled meaning Multiple Independently Targetable Re-entry Vehicles can be carried.
• Test conducted from Dr APJ Abdul Kalam Island also known as Wheeler Island in Odisha.
• India joins elite ICBM nations: USA, Russia, China, UK, and France.
• Range exceeds 10,000 km making any global target reachable.
• Defence Minister and PM congratulated DRDO scientists on the historic achievement.

articles.title: India GDP growth rate projected at 6.8% for FY 2026-27 by IMF
articles.summary:
• India remains the world fastest growing major economy for third consecutive year.
• Services sector contributes 55% to GDP while manufacturing stands at 28%.
• Private consumption growth is at 7.2% and government capex at Rs 11.21 lakh crore.
• Global growth is projected at 3.3% by IMF in World Economic Outlook 2026.
• India is ranked fastest growing among all G20 nations by IMF.

articles.title: Sebi’s new proposal enables mutual fund gifting through PPIs
articles.summary:
• Mutual fund investments can soon be gifted using prepaid payment instruments (PPIs), with Sebi proposing a new framework.
• Individuals can purchase gift PPIs, capped at ₹10,000 and valid for one year, to transfer funds for mutual fund subscriptions.
• This initiative aims to attract new investors and enhance financial product accessibility.

articles.title: Path ahead for Nepal’s new leadership
articles.summary:
• After securing a historic mandate in Nepal’s polls, Balendra Shah and the RSP face the task of navigating the West Asia conflict and managing ties with India, China and the U.S.
• While capitalising on old-guard fatigue proved an effective electoral strategy, the new leadership now faces a test in international relations.

articles.title: ‘US will never give up the Moon again’: Nasa unveils lunar base roadmap, nuclear Mars mission
articles.summary:
• NASA unveiled ambitious initiatives to accelerate its return to the Moon, aiming for a lunar landing by the end of President Trump's term and establishing a long-term presence.
• The agency's updated Moon strategy focuses on a phased approach to building a lunar base, incorporating commercial and international partnerships to support frequent crewed missions and sustained human….

articles.title: ‘Don’t just say ED, ED, ED’: Supreme Court questions West Bengal government in Mamata I-PAC raid case
articles.summary:
• The Supreme Court questioned West Bengal's objection to the ED's plea alleging obstruction by Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee during a raid.
• Justices emphasized the fundamental rights of individual ED officers, urging the state to focus on their alleged victimhood rather than just the agency.

articles.title: Metals melt as West Asia war rages on
articles.summary:
• Precious metals like gold and silver have seen significant price drops despite global uncertainties and inflation fears.
• This trend is attributed to a slowdown in de-dollarisation, potential interest rate hikes, central bank selling, and reduced industrial demand for silver.
• The war in West Asia has disrupted traditional market dynamics, leading to a bearish sentiment.

articles.title: NASA X-Ray Mission Gets Fresh Look at 2,000-Year-Old Supernova
articles.summary:
• NASA’s IXPE (Imaging X-ray Polarimetry Explorer) mission has taken a new observation of a supernova, RCW 86, helping fill in a fuller picture of what other telescopes have observed.
• When astronomers using NASA’s Chandra X-ray Observatory previously targeted RCW 86, they discovered that a large “cavity” region around the system led the supernova to expand […].

articles.title: NASA’s Water-Hunting Tool Will Help Scout Moon’s South Pole
articles.summary:
• NASA is joining international partners to hunt for ice on the Moon in support of future human exploration.
• The agency is providing a water-detecting instrument, the Neutron Spectrometer System (NSS), to the Lunar Polar Exploration (LUPEX) mission led by JAXA (Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency) and ISRO (Indian….

articles.title: Stock market today (March 24, 2026): Nifty50 ends above 22,900; BSE Sensex rises over 1,300 points as US-Iran war tensions ease
articles.summary:
• Stock market today: Nifty50 and BSE Sensex rallied in trade on Tuesday on hopes of easing tensions in the Middle East.
• While Nifty50 went above 23,000, BSE Sensex rose over 1,600 points intraday.

articles.title: NASA Selects Finalists in Student Aircraft Maintenance Competition
articles.summary:
• Editor’s note: This advisory was updated on March 24, 2026, to update the finalists list.

articles.title: 75L jobs added in informal sector in 2025: NSO survey
articles.summary:
• The 'other services' sector led growth with 35 lakh new jobs, while manufacturing and trade also saw significant additions.
• The number of informal establishments also grew by 8%.

articles.title: How the West Asian conflict upended global monetary policy
articles.summary:
• Major central banks have paused rate cuts due to the US-Iran war.
• RBI is facing a weakening rupee and risks to inflation, growth, and the current account, making its April policy decision tougher.

articles.title: India joins ITER thermonuclear fusion project as full member; MoU signed
articles.summary:
• MoU signed between Department of Atomic Energy India and ITER Organization.
• ITER is located in Cadarache France and aims to demonstrate fusion energy feasibility.
• 35 nations are participating including EU, USA, China, Russia, Japan, and South Korea.
• India will contribute 9% of total components worth USD 1.5 billion.
• Nuclear fusion produces 4 times more energy per kg than fission with minimal radioactive waste.
• This is a significant step for India clean energy ambitions.

articles.title: How agriPV can turn India’s farms into dual-purpose powerhouses
articles.summary:
• India’s ambitious energy transition goals — to have 300 GW of installed solar capacity by 2030 and achieve net-zero emissions by 2070 — put land at a premium.
• Utility-scale solar projects require large tracts of land while agriculture is already under pressure from competing land use.

articles.title: Tamil Nadu becomes first state to launch Universal Basic Income pilot
articles.summary:
• Rs 3,000 per month will be transferred directly to 10,000 beneficiary families.
• Districts covered include Villupuram, Krishnagiri, Nagapattinam, Tirunelveli, and Cuddalore.
• The pilot will run for 2 years and will be evaluated by IIT-Madras and NITI Aayog.
• World Bank is providing technical assistance for UBI pilot evaluation.
• It is the first such scheme launched by any Indian state government.

articles.title: D. Gukesh becomes youngest World Chess Champion at 18 defeating Ding Liren
articles.summary:
• He defeated reigning champion Ding Liren of China in a match held in Singapore.
• Gukesh won with 7.5 vs 6.5 points in the 14-game World Chess Championship 2024.
• He is the 18th World Chess Champion and second Indian after Viswanathan Anand.
• PM Modi congratulated Gukesh and Tamil Nadu government awarded Rs 5 crore.
• He is the youngest player ever to win the world title at age 18.
• FIDE headquarters is in Lausanne Switzerland.

articles.title: India launches National Quantum Mission with Rs 6,003 crore outlay for 8 years
articles.summary:
• Targets include developing 50-1000 qubit quantum computers and satellite-based quantum communication.
• Four Thematic Hubs cover quantum computing, quantum communication, sensing, and materials.
• Coordinated by Department of Science and Technology DST under Government of India.
• India joins USA, China, and Germany in top-tier national quantum mission countries.
• NQM will also create a quantum-trained workforce and protect critical infrastructure.

articles.title: India overtakes Japan to become 4th largest stock market globally
articles.summary:
• India total market cap stands at USD 5.2 trillion while Japan is at USD 5.1 trillion.
• Top 3 are USA at USD 48T, China at USD 9T, and UK at USD 3.4T.
• BSE Sensex crossed 82,000 and NSE Nifty 50 went above 25,000.
• India has 5,000+ listed companies on BSE which is the largest number in the world.
• FII net inflow in India equities was USD 28 billion in FY26.
articles.source: BSE / NSE / World Federation of Exchanges

articles.title: ECI makes Voter Verified Paper Audit Trail mandatory for all elections
articles.summary:
• All EVMs in general and state assembly elections must be paired with VVPAT machines.
• VVPAT allows voter to verify their vote via paper slip visible for 7 seconds.
• Supreme Court order upheld the ECI decision enhancing electoral transparency.
• India has used Electronic Voting Machines since 1982 and the first full EVM election was in 1999.
• ECI is a constitutional body established under Article 324 of the Constitution.

articles.title: India elected as non-permanent member of UN Security Council 2026-27
articles.summary:
• Secured 184 out of 193 votes in UN General Assembly elections for the Asia-Pacific seat.
• India will serve alongside Japan in the same group.
• This is India 8th term on UNSC since joining the UN in 1945.
• India has been campaigning for a permanent UNSC seat with veto power.
• UNSC has 5 permanent P5 members and 10 non-permanent members elected for 2-year terms.

articles.title: IIT Madras develops cheapest desalination technology at Rs 2 per litre
articles.summary:
• Technology is 80% cheaper than conventional reverse osmosis systems.
• Uses electrospun nanofiber membrane with graphene oxide coating.
• Can convert seawater and brackish water to potable water in 2 hours.
• Field trials in coastal Tamil Nadu showed 99.9% salt removal efficiency.
• Technology has been licensed to startup WaterGenics for commercial deployment.

articles.title: Oil shock, inflation pressures dampen RBI rate-cut hopes
articles.summary:
• Economists believe India's policy interest rate cuts are unlikely to continue due to West Asia crisis-driven inflation risks and the fading of a favorable base effect.
• Sustained foreign outflows and a weakening rupee could also force the Reserve Bank of India to reconsider further easing, potentially even a hike.

articles.title: West Asia conflict poses downside risk, India GDP growth seen at 7.1 pc in FY27: Crisil Intelligence
articles.summary:
• India's economy faces risks from West Asia conflict affecting oil prices.
• Real GDP growth is expected at 7.1 percent in FY27.
• This growth will be supported by consumer spending and investment.
• Exports are also set to grow.
• Retail inflation may rise to 4.3 percent.
• The Reserve Bank of India is likely to maintain interest rates.

articles.title: Conflict deepens health crisis across Middle East, WHO says
articles.summary:
• Health systems across the Region are coming under strain as injuries and displacement rise, attacks on health care continue, and public health risks increase.
• Health workers, patients and health facilities must always be protected under international humanitarian law.
• In Iran, WHO has verified 18 attacks on health care since 28 February, resulting in 8 deaths among health workers.
• National health authorities in Iran report more than 1300 deaths and 9000 injuries, and in Lebanon report at least 570 deaths and more than 1400 injuries.
• Over the same period in Lebanon, 25 attacks on health care have resulted in 16 deaths and 29 injuries.
• More than 50 emergency supply requests, intended to benefit over 1.5 million people across 25 countries, are affected, resulting in significant backlogs.

articles.title: Neeraj Chopra wins silver at World Athletics Championships 2025 in Tokyo
articles.summary:
• Gold went to Anderson Peters of Grenada with 91.23 m.
• Neeraj had won gold at World Athletics Championships 2023 in Budapest.

• India overall won 3 medals at the Championships comprising 1 silver and 2 bronze.

articles.title: The Commonwealth celebrates ‘Unlocking opportunities together’

• In London, a wreath-laying service was held at the Commonwealth Memorial Gates, commemorating more than five million Commonwealth soldiers who served and lost their lives during the First and Second World Wars.

• The audience at Westminster Abbey included His Majesty King Charles III, Her Majesty the Queen, foreign ministers, UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer, Commonwealth Secretary-General, Hon Shirley Botchwey, High Commissioners, senior government….
• The event will also mark the launch of the Commonwealth Small States Bulletin, highlighting practical interventions that support resilience and sustainable development in Commonwealth small states.  View a Flickr photo gallery of Commonwealth….

articles.title: Europeans cautious as they scramble to digest major US, Israeli attack on Iran
articles.summary:
• European nations are reacting to major strikes on Iran by the US and Israel.
• Leaders are working to safeguard their citizens in the Middle East.
• Germany is holding an emergency meeting.
• The European Union is evacuating some staff.
• France and Italy are urging caution for their citizens.
• The situation is described as perilous and a military escalation is underway.

articles.title: Chennai RWA shines in TN’s solar race
articles.summary:
• RWA is one of India's leading trade associations.
• Rwa is based in Chennai, Tamil Nadu, but also has offices in Mumbai and Pune.
• The RWA was founded in 1903 in Chennai and later expanded to other parts of the state.

articles.title: India wins 22 medals at 2026 Asian Winter Games in Harbin
articles.summary:
• The Indian contingent
        comprised 45 athletes participating in 8 disciplines.
• Speed skater Aryan Singh became the hero
        of the Games winning 3 gold medals.
• India finished 6th overall in the medal tally.
• China topped
        the medal tally with 87 gold medals.
• The 2026 Asian Winter Games were held from February 7-14, 2026.
• Union Sports Minister Mansukh
        Mandaviya congratulated the Indian athletes on their historic achievement.

articles.title: National Cancer Grid releases guidelines; AIIMS Delhi opens proton therapy centre
articles.summary:
• The National Cancer Grid (NCG), comprising 300+ cancer centres, has released updated
        treatment guidelines for 15 types of cancer.
• Simultaneously, AIIMS New Delhi has inaugurated
        India's first government-sector proton therapy centre, making advanced cancer treatment accessible
        at affordable cost.
• The centre can treat 25-30 patients daily with precise radiation targeting
        tumours while minimizing damage to surrounding tissue.
• Proton therapy costs Rs 15-20 lakh at AIIMS
        compared to Rs 50-70 lakh at private hospitals.
• India sees 14 lakh new cancer cases annually according to ICMR data.
• The National Cancer Mission
        has allocated Rs 5,000 crore for cancer infrastructure.

articles.title: India and France sign 5 MoUs including agreement on semiconductor technology
articles.summary:
• India and France have signed five Memoranda of Understanding (MoUs) during the bilateral
        summit between Prime Minister Narendra Modi and French President Emmanuel Macron in New Delhi.
• The agreements cover semiconductor technology, defence cooperation, renewable energy, digital
        infrastructure, and cultural exchanges.
• France has committed to invest Rs 10,000 crore in India's
        semiconductor ecosystem under the India Semiconductor Mission.
• France is India's 10th largest trading
        partner with bilateral trade worth USD 15 billion in FY25.
• A joint working group on cybersecurity
        was also established.
• India-France friendship dates back to 1998 Pokhran tests.

articles.title: RBI keeps repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for sixth consecutive time
articles.summary:
• The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) voted
        5:1 to hold the key policy rate.
• The RBI maintained its stance of 'withdrawal of accommodation'.
• GDP growth for FY26 projected at 6.8%.
• The Standing Deposit Facility (SDF) rate remains at 6.25%.
• The Marginal Standing Facility (MSF)
        rate and Bank Rate stand at 6.75%.
• Governor Sanjay Malhotra
        chaired the meeting held in Mumbai from February 5-7, 2026.

articles.title: India's Constitution Amendment Bill for 33% women reservation in Parliament passed
articles.summary:
• The Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam implementation process advanced as the Delimitation
        Commission released draft proposals for constituency boundaries.
• The constitutional amendment,
        passed in September 2023, reserves 33% seats for women in Lok Sabha and State Legislative
        Assemblies.
• Women currently constitute 14.4% of Lok Sabha members.
• Among states, Bihar has highest women
        representation.
• 106th Constitutional Amendment Act provides for sunset clause of 15 years.
• Several countries including Rwanda (61%), Sweden (46%) have higher women representation in Parliament.

articles.title: Union Budget 2026-27: Key highlights - Income tax exemption limit raised to Rs 12 lakh
articles.summary:
• Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman presented the Union Budget 2026-27 in Parliament.
• Capital expenditure allocation increased to Rs 11.21 lakh crore, up 10.7% from
        previous year.
• Agriculture sector gets Rs 1.52 lakh crore allocation.
• Education sector receives
        Rs 1.28 lakh crore.
• Infrastructure allocation
        includes Rs 2.5 lakh crore for railways.
• Defence budget increased to Rs 6.21 lakh crore.

articles.title: PM Modi inaugurates 10 new Vande Bharat Express trains; India now has 130+
articles.summary:
• New routes include Patna-Ranchi, Bhubaneswar-Kolkata, Dehradun-Delhi, Trichy-Chennai, and Nagpur-Mumbai.
• These semi-high-speed trains run at 160 kmph with automatic doors, GPS-based passenger information, and bio-vacuum toilets.
• Indian Railways aims to run 200 Vande Bharat trains by March 2026.
• The trains are manufactured at Integral Coach Factory ICF Chennai and Modern Coach Factory MCF Raebareli.
• With this launch India has over 130 Vande Bharat trains operational nationwide.

articles.title: India's forex reserves touch all-time high of USD 720 billion in January 2026
articles.summary:
• This marks
        an increase of USD 5.93 billion from the previous week.
• Foreign Currency Assets (FCA), the largest
        component of reserves, stood at USD 641.50 billion.
• Gold reserves increased to USD 68.47 billion.
• Special Drawing Rights (SDRs) with IMF stood at USD 5.97 billion.
• India's Reserve Tranche Position
        with IMF was USD 4.33 billion.
• At current import rate, India's reserves can cover 11.5 months
        of imports.

articles.title: ISRO successfully launches NVS-02 navigation satellite aboard GSLV-F15
articles.summary:
• NVS-02 is the second satellite of the NavIC (Navigation with Indian Constellation)
        second-generation series.
• The satellite will strengthen India's independent regional navigation
        system.
• NVS-02 weighs approximately 2,250 kg and carries L1, L5, and S-band navigation payloads.
• GSLV-F15 is the 17th flight of GSLV and 11th with indigenous cryogenic stage.
• The NavIC system
        provides positioning services with 10 metre accuracy over India and 1,500 km beyond its borders.

articles.title: GST Council reduces tax rate on life insurance premiums to 5%
articles.summary:
• Term life insurance policies will attract NIL GST.
• Health insurance premiums for
        senior citizens above 60 years exempted from GST.
• The Council also approved uniform GST rate of
        5% on all fortified rice.
• GST on used EVs set at 18% (was 12%).
• The total GST revenue in January 2026 stood at Rs 1.96 lakh crore, 12.6% higher YoY.
• All states and Centre are members with Centre having 1/3 voting power.

articles.title: Guyana elections seen as fair, but inclusivity challenges remain - Commonwealth observer report on Guyana’s 2025 elections
articles.summary:

• The Group noted persistent challenges, which affected public confidence and equity, including uneven access to campaign resources.

Read the full report Related news Commonwealth to observe Guyana’s 2025 general and regional elections Commonwealth Observer Group….

• The Group in its engagement noted gaps in inclusive participation: youth, persons with disabilities, and Amerindian communities were underrepresented, and the Guyana Elections Commission (GECOM) lacked sex-disaggregated data for polling staff and candidates, which limited transparency in planning….

articles.title: IIT Bombay develops AI model to decode satellite images using natural language
articles.summary:
• IIT Bombay developing AI model that can read satellite images from natural language using artificial intelligence.
• The model will be used to help scientists understand satellite images of the Earth from space.
• The project is being funded by the Indian Institutes of Information Technology (IITs) and the Indian Reserve Bank of India (IRBIs).

articles.title: Submission deadline for Commonwealth Peace Prize extended until January 2025
articles.summary:
• Submission deadline for Commonwealth Peace Prize extended until January 2025 M.Muchiri Thu, 12/19/2024 - 16:27 19 December 2024 News Commonwealth Secretariat Image The announcement of the first-ever Commonwealth Peace.
• Prize has led to an overwhelming response, and eligible entrants now have until 17 January 2025 to submit nominations for the inaugural award.
• The winner will be announced at a special ceremony during Commonwealth Week in March 2025.
• Announcing the extension at a briefing of High Commissioners and Commonwealth Accredited Organisations in London on 6 December 2024,  the Commonwealth Secretary-General, the Rt Hon Patricia Scotland KC, underscored the role of peace in achieving fair, just and peaceful societies.
• We hope it will inspire people across the Commonwealth and beyond to employ faith as an influencer of peace and harmony.” The Commonwealth Peace Prize was launched on the International Day of Peace 2024 and aims to recognise distinguished peacebuilders with a strong track record of….
• Contact your High Commission in London or a Commonwealth Accredited Organisation to submit an official nomination on your behalf.

articles.title: Teen Innovator Soars to New Heights: Mehar Singh Breaks Guinness World Record with Lightning-Fast Drone Ascent
articles.summary:
• Singh also breaks record for fastest drone descent by a teenager.
• Singh breaks record by flying drone at speeds of up to 50mph.
• He also breaks world record with fastest drone ascent by a teen in the U.S.

articles.title: Implementation of 'guarantees', decline of BRS favour Congress in Telangana, BJP aiming for better show
articles.summary:
• The BJP is riding high on its growing voter base in Telangana.
• The party doubled its vote share to nearly 14 per cent resulting in eight seats in the assembly elections held on November 30, last year.
• BRS, which ruled the state for about a decade since its emergence, is low on morale following the defeat.
• Its founder and former Chief Minister K Chandrasekhar Rao's daughter K Kavitha was arrested on the eve of poll dates announcement.
• The BJP, riding high on its growing voter base in Telangana, is now aiming to win over 12 out of the total 17 seats and 35 per cent vote share, in the upcoming Lok Sabha polls.

articles.title: This new AI tool can help you book train tickets, get refunds and check details on IRCTC website and app
articles.summary:
• It can also help you check details of train tickets and get refunds.
• The tool is available for iOS and Android users and is available on Google Play and the App Store.
• For more information on the tool, visit theIRCTC.com/AI-Ticket.

articles.title: Why Earth Sciences minister Rijiju is upset with this European IT company
articles.summary:
• The company is based in Brussels, Belgium, but has offices in the Netherlands and Germany.
• The minister is upset because the company has been taking advantage of the country's IT infrastructure.
• The government is also upset because of the company's high cost of doing business in the country.
• The firm is also based in Paris and has offices around the world.

articles.title: LJP implodes as uncle Paras isolates Chirag Paswan; Nitish Kumar's JD(U) says 'you reap what you sow'
articles.summary:
• In a surprise turn of events, Chirag Paswan, the president of Lok Janshakti Party (LJP) founded by his father late Ram Vilas Paswan, was on Monday unceremoniusly unseated as the leader of the party in Lok Sabha.
• Chirag, who took over the mantle of the party in 2020 after the death of Ram Vilas Paswan, stood completely isolated at the top within his party.

articles.title: Bengal extends Covid-19 curbs till July 1: All you need to know
articles.summary:
• West Bengal government on Monday extended the ongoing Covid-19 restrictions in the state till July 1.
• Essential services will continue operating following protocols, the chief minister Mamata Banerjee said.
• West Bengal's Covid-19 tally rose to 14,61,257 on Sunday as 3,984 more people tested positive for the infection, while 84 fresh fatalities pushed the state's coronavirus death toll to 16,896.

articles.title: Time for government, companies to adopt artificial intelligence: Deloitte India
articles.summary:
• “Indian companies need to re-skill, train, and acquire more relevant talent, if they are to successfully integrate AI technologies.
• Most importantly, they should be willing to make the continued investment.
• It is an uphill race in which there will be winners and losers,” said Deloitte India CEO N Venkatram.

articles.title: Inflation reflects growth dynamics in India: Christopher Wood
articles.summary:
• Inflation is a reflection of India's growth dynamics, says Wood.
• India's inflation rate is at an all-time high of 7.7 per cent, according to the Reserve Bank of India.
• In the past year, the rate has risen to 7.8 per cent.
• In 2013, it hit a record high of 8.9 per cent in the month of March.

articles.title: Fin Crisis: Too late and too little done in US
articles.summary:
• U.S. faces a financial crisis that threatens to engulf the entire world.
• The crisis has already claimed the lives of more than 100,000 people in the past year.
• Has set up a fund to help the victims of the financial crisis.
• And has a total of $1.2 billion in assets.

articles.title: Weak rupee takes its toll on cos with huge foreign debt

• Many of these firms have to rely on the rupee to make ends meet.
